mission: Implement a repository-native software experiment compiler that turns exact repository facts and a task into typed representations, verified transformations, a task capsule, proof-carrying claims, an admitted plan, executable evaluation, and calibrated outcomes for any coding agent.
authority: user-supplied Repository-Native Software Experiment Compiler design
observed: The repository already distinguishes P0 representation calculations, P1 formal model-property certificates, P2 representation fidelity, P3 executable software evidence, and P4 external acceptance.
The prior implementation was operationally incomplete because active governance relied primarily on prose and string-presence checks.

The repository has no executable repository-fact extractor, representation registry, source-to-model correspondence verifier, claim-certificate verifier, task capsule compiler, experiment chronology controller, or query interface.
Trace012 is complete historical UI authority; this methodology mutation requires a new transaction rather than silently changing active authority while no transaction is open.
unknown: retrospective recall and precision across Trace007 through Trace012 until benchmark execution
prospective improvement over the existing evidence-and-invariants workflow until matched task outcomes exist
which future specialized dialects will outperform the implemented base cascade
hard_invariants: facts are content-addressed and tied to an exact Git SHA
every retained representation declares generator, schema, provenance, assumptions, validation, proof class, and correspondence boundary
P1 claims require a machine-checkable certificate and small verifier
P1 model proof never silently promotes itself through P2, P3, or P4
task capsule and predictions predate source edits and outcomes
every hard invariant hyperedge has an accepted proof obligation or explicit blocker
changed paths are covered by generated impact analysis or explicit discovered-later evidence
specialized representations activate through an admissibility selector rather than every method running on every task
no remote mutable skill or task-time package download is required
the engine remains vendor-neutral for Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, Pi, local models, deterministic scripts, and humans
selected_vertical_slice: normalized hypergraph spectral certificate with checked residual
non_goals: claim that the experiment compiler has already improved engineering outcomes
install MLIR, Souffle, egglog, an SMT solver, a theorem prover, or numerical packages as runtime dependencies
implement every future representation dialect in the first vertical slice
claim managed database, provider, target-host, accessibility, pilot, deployment, or production acceptance
